user_query:
I need a reproducible, timestamped snapshot of my workstation status and a reference web page for a policy brief. Please create the following deliverables under the project root:

1) outputs/system_report.json
- Include: os_name, os_version, python_version, cpu_count, workspace_path (absolute), disk_total_bytes and disk_free_bytes for the filesystem containing the workspace_path, input_dir set to "input", input_file_count, input_total_size_bytes, inventory_csv_path (relative path to the CSV in item 2), generated_at_utc (ISO 8601), and a web_snapshot object with fields: resolved_url, http_status, content_length, sha256, title (or null), link_count (count of <a> tags), canonical_href (or null).
- Cross-checks that must hold: (a) input_total_size_bytes equals the sum of size_bytes in outputs/input_inventory.csv; (b) web_snapshot.sha256 exactly matches the SHA-256 of outputs/web_snapshot/who_homepage.html and the sha256 recorded in outputs/web_snapshot/metadata.json.

2) outputs/input_inventory.csv
- Inspect the local directory "input/" (recursively) and list one row per file found.
- Columns: path (relative, using forward slashes), size_bytes (integer), sha256 (hex), is_text (true/false, based on simple content sniffing), first_80_chars (first 80 visible characters if is_text is true, else empty).

3) outputs/web_snapshot/who_homepage.html and outputs/web_snapshot/metadata.json
- Download the landing HTML of the official World Health Organization institutional homepage (the root page on the who.int domain). Save the raw HTML bytes to outputs/web_snapshot/who_homepage.html.
- Extract and save to outputs/web_snapshot/metadata.json: resolved_url (after any redirects), http_status, content_length (bytes of the saved file), sha256 (hex of the saved file), title (text of <title>, or null), link_count (number of <a ...> tags), canonical_href (href of <link rel="canonical"> if present, else null), and fetched_at_utc (ISO 8601).

Constraints and notes:
- Do not use private or privileged tools; rely on standard OS, shell, or scripting utilities/libraries available in a typical user environment.
- All paths in the JSON and CSV should be correct and usable from the project root.
- Ensure the report is self-consistent: the SHA-256 and sizes recorded must match the actual saved files.

Inputs are in the "input/" directory. Please make sure all three deliverables are generated exactly at the specified locations. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
